# High-End AI-Generated UI Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Implement the high-end, cinematic frontend design concepts generated by the AI (Dark Product mode, double-bezel cards, ruby-red accents, and liquid glass headers) into the React application.

**Architecture:** We will update the global CSS tokens to enforce a deep charcoal palette and strict typography. We will then refactor the main layout (`App.tsx`) to use clean dividers instead of boxy cards, apply the "Doppelrand" nested architecture to the `LoginGate.tsx`, and polish the `LeadTable.tsx` and `MessageBuilder.tsx` to match the generated premium aesthetic.

**Tech Stack:** React, TypeScript, standard CSS variables (vanilla CSS).

---

### Task 1: CSS Tokens & Core Aesthetics

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/index.css`
- Modify: `index.html`

- [ ] **Step 1: Update CSS Variables and Utilities**

Replace the `:root` variables in `src/index.css` to use the premium Ruby accent and deep charcoal backgrounds, and add the `liquid-glass` and `doppel-shell` utility classes.

```css
:root {
  --bg-base: #050507;
  --bg-surface: #0a0a0e;
  --bg-raised: #111116;
  --bg-elevated: #18181f;
  --accent: #E33A52;
  --text-primary: #F3F4F6;
  --text-muted: #6B7280;
  /* ... other standard tokens ... */
}

.liquid-glass {
  background: rgba(10, 10, 14, 0.65);
  backdrop-filter: blur(24px) saturate(180%);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06);
}

.doppel-shell {
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.02);
  border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.04);
  padding: 6px;
  border-radius: 24px;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add theme-color to HTML**

Update `index.html` to include the dark theme color.

```html
<meta name="theme-color" content="#050507" />
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add src/index.css index.html
git commit -m "feat: implement high-end CSS tokens and glass utilities"
```

---

### Task 2: Layout & Dashboard Structure

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/App.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Refactor App Layout**

Update `src/App.tsx` to remove generic `<Card>` wrappers in the sidebar, replacing them with clean `border-top` dividers and applying the `.liquid-glass` class to the header.

```tsx
<header className="liquid-glass" style={{ height: 64, padding: '0 32px' }}>
  {/* Header content */}
</header>
<aside style={{ width: 380, background: 'var(--bg-base)', borderRight: '1px solid var(--border-medium)' }}>
  <div style={{ padding: '32px 24px' }}>
    <UploadZone />
  </div>
  <div style={{ padding: '32px 24px', borderTop: '1px solid var(--border-subtle)' }}>
    <CampaignPanel />
  </div>
</aside>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add src/App.tsx
git commit -m "feat: restructure dashboard layout with clean dividers and glass header"
```

---

### Task 3: Secure Operator Login Terminal

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/components/LoginGate.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement Cinematic Glow & Doppelrand Card**

Add a massive radial blur background and nest the login form inside a double-bezel card structure. Add active scale physics to the Enter button.

```tsx
{/* Cinematic Ambient Glow */}
<div style={{
  position: 'absolute', width: '60vw', height: '60vw',
  background: 'radial-gradient(circle, rgba(227, 58, 82, 0.08) 0%, transparent 70%)',
  filter: 'blur(40px)', pointerEvents: 'none',
}} />

<div className="doppel-shell">
  <div className="doppel-core" style={{ padding: '48px 40px' }}>
    {/* Login Inputs */}
  </div>
</div>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add src/components/LoginGate.tsx
git commit -m "feat: upgrade login terminal to cinematic operator aesthetic"
```

---

### Task 4: WhatsApp Chat Bubble Preview

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/components/MessageBuilder.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Style Native WhatsApp Bubble**

Refactor the preview container to look like a physical WhatsApp message bubble with a deep green background `#005c4b`, a top-left chat tail, and embedded images.

```tsx
<div style={{ background: '#005c4b', borderRadius: '0 8px 8px 8px', position: 'relative', padding: '4px' }}>
  <div style={{ position: 'absolute', top: 0, left: -8, borderTop: '8px solid #005c4b', borderLeft: '8px solid transparent' }} />
  {/* Images and text inside bubble */}
</div>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add src/components/MessageBuilder.tsx
git commit -m "feat: implement native WhatsApp bubble styling for preview"
```

---

### Task 5: Premium Table & Input States

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/components/LeadTable.tsx`
- Modify: `src/components/CampaignPanel.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Refine Table Sent State**

In `LeadTable.tsx`, remove the row opacity drop. Instead, apply a strikethrough to the name and a subtle success-tinted background for sent rows.

```tsx
<span style={{ textDecoration: isSent ? 'line-through' : 'none', opacity: isSent ? 0.6 : 1 }}>
  {row.original.name}
</span>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Refine Input Fields**

In `CampaignPanel.tsx`, add inner shadows to inputs and apply a Ruby ring on focus.

```tsx
const inputStyle = {
  background: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.02)',
  boxShadow: 'inset 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.2)',
  transition: 'all 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)',
};
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Run Build to Verify**

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: PASS with no TypeScript errors.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add src/components/LeadTable.tsx src/components/CampaignPanel.tsx
git commit -m "feat: refine data table rows and input focus states"
```
